Title: Hydrophobic recognition allows the glycosyltransferase UGT76G1 to catalyze its substrate in two orientations

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Steviol glycoside glycosylation branch; β(1–3) glucosylation at R1 and R2 ends
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 3-hydroxyl of glucose A attached to the steviol aglycone, forming a β(1–3) glycosidic linkage on steviol glycosides at either the R1 (C13-linked glycone) or R2 (C19-linked glycone) position.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: UDP-glucose; steviol glycosides containing a steviol aglycone plus glucose A with a free 3-hydroxyl (including steviolbioside, stevioside, rebaudioside A, rebaudioside D, rebaudioside E, rubusoside, and self-made R1/R2 monoglucosides)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Rebaudioside B; Rebaudioside A; Rebaudioside I; Rebaudioside D; Rebaudioside M; glycosylated rubusoside products RX and RY
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural zero-calorie sweeteners; Reb D and especially Reb M have improved sweetness quality with reduced bitter aftertaste and are desirable sugar substitutes.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and also forms immediate precursor/product series toward Reb D and Reb M by β(1–3) glucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream glycosylation step; branch elaboration step acting on both R1 and R2 ends
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for formation of desired sweet steviol glycosides; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of preferred-taste steviol glycosides by catalyzing β(1–3) glucosylation, including conversion steps leading to Reb D and Reb M; R1 transfer is faster than R2 transfer.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays; HPLC and high-resolution MS/MS product identification; steady-state kinetics; X-ray crystallography of UDP/Reb A and UDP/Rubu complexes; mutagenesis (H25A, H25N)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase; GT-B fold glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Structural data: Protein Data Bank accession codes 6INF; 6ING; 6INH; 6INI
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with UDP-glucose; crystallization with UDP and steviol glucosides; no significant effect of Mg2+ addition or EDTA removal of Mg2+
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: R2 glucosylation is kinetically slower than R1; no broader organismal tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering or deployment in Stevia or microbial production systems to increase high-value sweeteners Reb D and Reb M; hydrophobic acceptor recognition suggests engineering opportunities for broader glycoconjugate synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT76G1 catalyzes β(1–3) glucosylation of steviol glycosides using UDP-glucose. 2. The enzyme modifies both asymmetric R1 and R2 ends of the steviol aglycone, but R1 glucosylation precedes and is kinetically favored over R2 glucosylation. 3. Structural and mutational analyses show that hydrophobic recognition of the steviol aglycone permits two substrate orientations while His25 is essential for catalysis of both ends. 4. UGT76G1 is crucial for biosynthesis of desirable sweeteners such as Reb D and Reb M and can even glycosylate an artificial hydrophobic glucoside acceptor.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The enzyme does not glycosylate free glucose, sophorose alone, or steviol aglycone alone, indicating that both a hydrophobic aglycone and an acceptor glucose with free 3-OH are required. The same active site processes both R1 and R2 through normal and flipped binding modes. Reb D and Reb M production is economically important because R2 sugars transferred by UGT76G1 contribute to improved sweetness without bitterness.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds the first glucose with a β-glycosidic linkage to the C13-hydroxyl (R1) position of steviol aglycone.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream UGT76G1-dependent sweet steviol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates the R1 monoglucoside precursor required for subsequent β(1–3) glucosylation by UGT76G1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant expression and enzymatic synthesis of self-made steviol monoglucoside used in downstream UGT76G1 assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with downstream glycosyltransferases to reconstruct or engineer steviol glycoside pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT85C2 places the initial glucose at the R1 position of steviol aglycone. 2. In this study it was used to synthesize an R1 monoglucoside substrate for testing UGT76G1. 3. Its product confirms that a single glucose at R1 is sufficient for further β(1–3) glucosylation by UGT76G1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene served mainly as a pathway-supporting enzyme in substrate preparation rather than the primary mechanistic focus of the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds the first glucose with a β-glycosidic linkage to the C19-carboxylate (R2) position of steviol aglycone.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream UGT76G1-dependent R2 β(1–3) glucosylation and production of desired sweeteners
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates the R2 monoglucoside precursor that can be further glucosylated by UGT76G1, though more slowly than R1 substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant expression and enzymatic synthesis of self-made steviol monoglucoside used in downstream UGT76G1 assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with UGT76G1 and other steviol pathway UGTs to engineer high-value glycoside profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74G1 places the initial glucose at the R2 position of steviol aglycone. 2. In this study it was used to synthesize an R2 monoglucoside substrate for testing UGT76G1. 3. Its product demonstrates that the R2 monoglucoside is also a valid, though less efficient, acceptor for UGT76G1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene served mainly as a pathway-supporting enzyme in substrate preparation rather than the primary mechanistic focus of the paper.
